subject: [SDNC/CCSDK] Guilin version bump
commit_message:
[SDNC/CCSDK] Guilin version bump

Bump image versions to use Guilin M4 version of SDNC images and of
CCSDK dgbuilder.  Note - SDNR remains disabled in this commit pending
fix to its endpoint.  Also, note that CDS version bump is not included -
that will be submitted as a separate review.

Issue-ID: SDNC-1355
Signed-off-by: Dan Timoney <dtimoney@att.com>
Change-Id: I8d660a8e303a18d4674f832b9239fa50c5abf3f2

it seems that the new image from SDNC needs more RAM (https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112626-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-sdnc-0-sdnc-logs.html)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> it seems that the new image from SDNC needs more RAM (https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112626-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-sdnc-0-sdnc-logs.html)
> please also update request/limits

Looking into this ... I think actually the problem has to do with the file used to tune Java settings (setenv), so the fix will almost certainly include a new version of that file.  Also will verify that request/limits match up with our tuning (we need to tune heap size on the java command line, and also make sure we're not asking for more heap than the limit).

Also will verify that request/limits match up with our tuning (we need to tune heap size on the java command line, and also make sure we're not asking for more heap than the limit).

There was a typo in the setenv file, which was preventing the garbage collection tuning settings from being used.  That is the most likely cause of this error.  I reviewed the resource limits and they seem okay
